english: Lex. being untrue in one's dealings, acting wrongfully, which also my referees confirmed to be the general import of the word; in books, however, it is usually joined to ọdód-pas (ʼdod pas), or ọdód-pa-la (ʼdod pa la), adding lóg-par (log par), as: ọdód-pa-la lóg-par ɣyém-pa (ʼdod pa la log par g.yem pa), or it stands alone as in ɣyém byéd-pa (g.yem byed pa), signifying 'to commit adultery, fornication' Dzl. and elsewh.; log-ɣyém (log g.yem) sbst. —
(vulg. *er-kʼa* (*er kha*)), bell, set of bells, or peal Glr.
Lex., Sch.: a light, luminous place.
Med. frq., Guinea pepper, Capsicum W. *nyér-ma* (*nyer ma*); ɣyer-šiṅ-pa (g.yer shing pa) medicinal herb S.g.

1. to be idle, lazy, slothful; idleness, laziness; ɣyel-ba-méd-par (g.yel ba med par) incessantly, continually, e. g. to pray, to guard Mil., S.O. — 2. tʼugs ɣyél-ba (thugs g.yel ba) resp. to forget W.
(rarely yo (yo)) craft, cunning, deceit, more frq. ɣyo-sgyú (g.yo sgyu), ɣyo-zól (g.yo zol); ɣyó-ċan (g.yo can) crafty, deceitful, ɣyo-méd (g.yo med) honest, ɣyo byéd-pa (g.yo byed pa) to deceive.

I. vb, pf. and imp. ɣyos (g.yos), 1. to move, to cause to change place; to be moved, agitated, shaken, ɣnam sa ɣyós-so (gnam sa g.yos so) heaven and earth were shaken Dzl.; des ni sa ọdi ɣyo-bar ọgyur (des ni sa ʼdi g.yo bar ʼgyur) thereby the earth may be shaken Do.; to bend, incline, tilt, e.g. a vessel; *zúg-po yos toṅ* (*zug po yos tong*) W. make a bow! sku ɣyós-par ọgyúr-to (sku g.yos par ʼgyur to) the image began to move Glr.; sa-ɣyós (sa g.yos) earthquake; to begin to move or to march Ma.; tʼugs-rje ɣtiṅ-nas ɣyós-pai rtags (thugs rje gting nas g.yos pa'i rtags) it is a sign that his heart is moved by grace Mil. nt.; dgé-bai pʼyogs-la ọdu-šés ċuṅ-zad kyaṅ ma ɣyos (dge ba'i phyogs la ʼdu shes cung zad kyang ma g.yos) he did not allow the least virtuous impulses to rise (in his heart), he kept down every sense of virtue; ɣyó-ba (g.yo ba) partic., continually moving, restless, uneasy, of the mind Mil., mi-ɣyó-ba (mi g.yo ba) unmoved, immovable, n. of Siva and of other terrifying deities Glr. (cf. अचल Will) — 2. to prepare, victuals for the table ɣyós-su byéd-paid. (g.yos su byed pa id.); ɣyós-kʼaṅ (g.yos khang) kitchen, bake-house, ɣyós-mkʼan (g.yos mkhan) baker, cook. II. sbst. moveableness, mobility, yáṅ-žiṅ ɣyo-ba-nyíd (yang zhing g.yo ba nyid) an easy mobility Wdn.

(v. yog (yog), ọg (ʼog)) Tar. and elsewh., usually occurring in the more definite form ɣyóg-po (g.yog po), servant, man-servant, ɣyóg-mo (g.yog mo) maid-servant, female servant, waiting-maid; when distinguished from kól-po (khol po), kól-mo (khol mo) and bran (bran), it denotes a higher degree, e.g. ɣyog-mo ɣnyis (g.yog mo gnyis) two waiting-maids and besides 500 kól-mo (khol mo) maid-servants Pth.; ɣyóg-po daṅ yáṅ-ɣyog daṅ nyíṅ-ɣyog (g.yog po dang yang g.yog dang nying g.yog) servant, servant's servant, and the servant again of these Pth.; mii ɣyog byéd-pa (mi'i g.yog byed pa) to be in a person's service, to obey a person; dpon-ɣyóg (dpon g.yog) master (mistress) and domestics, master and attendants, frq.; nad-ɣyóg (nad g.yog), a nurse, one that tends sick persons Dzl.; ɣyog-ọkór (g.yog ʼkhor) attendants, e.g. ɣyog-ọkór bċu-drúg (g.yog ʼkhor bcu drug) attendants and retinue of 16 persons, ọkór daṅ ɣyog (ʼkhor dang g.yog) id.

pf. and imp. ɣyogs (g.yogs), rarely yóg-pa (yog pa) 1. to cover, bu gós-kyis ɣyóg-pa (bu gos kyis g.yog pa) to cover a child with a garment Dzl., mgó-la rdzá-ma ɣyóg-pa (mgo la rdza ma g.yog pa) to cover one's head with a pot Glr.; also: rdzá-mai mgó-la drá-bas ɣyóg-pa (rdza ma'i mgo la dra bas g.yog pa) to cover the opening of a pot with a wire grate Glr.; pʼyii págs-pa ɣyogs (phyi'i pags pa g.yogs) the external cutaneous covering appears (in the embryo) S.g.; ri-mgo kʼa-bas ɣyogs (ri mgo kha bas g.yogs) the hill-tops were covered with snow Mil.; to pour over or upon, to cover in pouring, kʼrág-gis (khrag gis) with blood Dzl.; to overlay, with gold Dzl.; to sprinkle over, besprinkle, *sig-pa-la ṭ́ág* (*sig pa la khrag*) W. the wall with blood; to strew over, *kʼá-la gog-tʼál* (*kha la gog thal*) W. ashes over the snow. — 2. to pour away, to throw away; so W.; the people in W. understand the words Dzl. 72, 6: *ma yógs-pai lḥág-ma* (*ma yogs pa'i lhag ma*) the rest which has not been thrown away, whereas others, e.g. the people of Sikkim explain it: the rest that has not been taken possession or care of.
1. cover, covering, mgo-ɣyógs (mgo g.yogs) Lex. covering for the head, cap; also fig. and po. for self-delusion, self-de-
